data.title: Crescent Capital BDC Reports Q3 2025 Earnings
data.description:
Crescent Capital Bdc, Inc. ( (CCAP) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Crescent Capital Bdc, Inc. presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Crescent Capital BDC, Inc. is a business development company focused on providing capital solutions to middle-market companies, aiming for current income and capital appreciation. In its third quarter of 2025 earnings report, Crescent Capital BDC announced a net investment income of $0.46 per share and a net income of $0.19 per share, with a net asset value (NAV) per share of $19.28. The company declared a fourth quarter base dividend of $0.42 per share.

The company’s financial highlights for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, include investments valued at $1,580.7 million and total assets of $1,627.6 million. Crescent Capital BDC reported a decrease in investment income to $41.4 million from the previous quarter’s $43.0 million, attributed to a decline in benchmark rates and restructuring of certain debt investments. The company maintained a weighted average yield on income-producing securities at 10.4%.

Crescent Capital BDC’s portfolio activity for the quarter involved investing $73.9 million across seven new portfolio companies and several follow-on revolver and delayed draw fundings, while exiting, selling, and repaying $86.0 million in aggregate. The company’s debt to equity ratio stood at 1.23x, with $27.8 million in cash and cash equivalents and $239.8 million of undrawn capacity on its credit facilities.

Looking ahead, Crescent Capital BDC’s management remains focused on leveraging its extensive experience and disciplined investment process to navigate the current economic environment and continue providing value to its shareholders. The company aims to sustain its strategic investments and maintain its strong position in the market.

data.title: The Oncology Institute Reports Strong Q3 2025 Growth
data.description:
Oncology Institute, Inc. ( (TOI) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Oncology Institute, Inc. presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

The Oncology Institute, Inc. (NASDAQ: TOI) is a leading value-based community oncology group in the United States, providing specialized cancer care through a network of clinics and affiliate locations across multiple states. In its third quarter of 2025, the company reported a significant increase in consolidated revenue, reaching $136.6 million, a 36.7% rise compared to the same period in 2024. This growth was driven by a 13% increase in fee-for-service revenue and record-setting performance in its retail pharmacy and dispensary sectors.

Key financial metrics for the quarter included a gross profit of $18.9 million, marking a 31.7% increase, and a net loss of $16.5 million, slightly higher than the previous year’s loss of $16.1 million. The company also reported an improvement in adjusted EBITDA, which was $(3.5) million compared to $(8.2) million in the prior year. The Oncology Institute’s cash and cash equivalents stood at $27.7 million as of September 30, 2025.

Strategically, the company expanded its network by signing new in-network MSO providers in Florida and opening a new pharmacy location in the state. Additionally, the appointment of Kristin England as Chief Administrative Officer is expected to enhance the company’s operational efficiency and technology strategy.

Looking ahead, The Oncology Institute has updated its full-year 2025 guidance, projecting revenue between $495 to $505 million and maintaining its gross profit forecast. The company anticipates adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $(11) to $(13) million, reflecting its confidence in continued growth and operational improvements. Despite the challenges, the company remains committed to advancing value-based oncology care while driving sustainable growth.

data.title: BioAtla Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results and Progress
data.description:
Bioatla, Inc. ( (BCAB) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Bioatla, Inc. presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

BioAtla, Inc. is a global clinical-stage biotechnology company specializing in the development of Conditionally Active Biologic (CAB) antibody therapeutics for treating solid tumors, utilizing its proprietary CAB platform technology to enhance selectivity and efficacy in cancer treatment.

The company recently reported its third-quarter financial results for 2025, highlighting significant progress in its clinical programs, including FDA alignment on a Phase 3 trial for Ozuriftamab Vedotin (Oz-V) and advancements in strategic partnerships.

Key financial metrics revealed a decrease in research and development expenses to $9.5 million, down from $16.4 million in the previous year, attributed to program prioritization and workforce reductions. General and administrative expenses also decreased, reflecting cost-saving measures. Despite these reductions, the company reported a net loss of $15.8 million for the quarter, primarily due to the absence of collaboration revenue recorded in the prior year.

Strategically, BioAtla is advancing its Oz-V trial for 2L+ OPSCC, aiming for accelerated approval, and is progressing with its BA3182 trial in advanced adenocarcinomas. The company is also on track to finalize a strategic transaction with a potential partner by year-end, which could bolster its financial and operational position.

Looking forward, BioAtla’s management remains optimistic about the potential of its CAB platform and ongoing clinical trials, with expectations of continued progress and strategic partnerships to drive future growth and innovation in cancer therapeutics.

data.title: Copel Reports Mixed Q3 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Companhia Paranaense De Energia Pfd Class B ( (ELP) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Companhia Paranaense De Energia Pfd Class B presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Companhia Paranaense De Energia, also known as Copel, is a Brazilian company operating in the energy sector, primarily engaged in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. It is recognized for its diversified energy portfolio and extensive transmission network across Brazil.

In its latest earnings report for the third quarter of 2025, Copel reported a recurring EBITDA of R$ 1.3 billion, marking a 7.8% increase compared to the same period last year. The company also highlighted a recurring net income of R$ 374.8 million for the quarter. Copel’s strategic initiatives included the optimization of its portfolio through acquisitions and divestments, as well as advancements in its investment programs aimed at modernizing its infrastructure.

Key financial metrics showed a mixed performance. While recurring EBITDA saw an increase, the overall EBITDA declined by 11% year-over-year. Net profit also decreased significantly by 68.5% compared to the previous year. The company’s leverage ratio stood at 2.8x, excluding the effects of recent acquisitions, indicating a stable financial position despite the increased debt levels. Copel’s operating revenue grew by 18.7%, driven by higher electricity supply revenue and increased construction activities.

Copel’s investment program for the quarter amounted to R$ 981.4 million, with significant allocations towards improving its distribution grid and enhancing its transmission lines. The company continued to focus on renewable energy sources, with a significant portion of its generation capacity derived from hydro and wind power.

Looking ahead, Copel remains committed to its strategic goals of enhancing operational efficiency and expanding its energy portfolio. The company aims to continue its investment in infrastructure modernization and renewable energy projects, positioning itself for sustainable growth in the evolving energy market.

data.title: Star Equity Holdings Reports Strong Q3 2025 Results
data.description:

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions

Star Equity Holdings, Inc., formerly known as Hudson Global, Inc., is a diversified holding company that focuses on acquiring, managing, and growing businesses with strong fundamentals across four segments: Building Solutions, Business Services, Energy Services, and Investments. The company aims to build long-term shareholder value through strategic acquisitions and operational excellence.

In its latest earnings report for the third quarter of 2025, Star Equity Holdings reported a significant increase in revenue and gross profit, driven largely by a recent merger. Despite a net loss, the company showed improvement in adjusted net income per diluted share, indicating a positive shift in profitability. The report highlighted the company’s strategic focus on expanding its market presence and enhancing operational efficiencies.

Key financial metrics from the third quarter include a 30.1% increase in revenue to $48.0 million and a 10.9% rise in gross profit to $20.6 million compared to the same period last year. The company reported an adjusted EBITDA of $1.3 million, up from $0.8 million in the previous year. The Building Solutions and Energy Services segments showed strong pro forma growth, while the Business Services segment maintained steady performance despite industry challenges.

The company continues to focus on strategic growth through share repurchases and a new acquisition strategy, aiming to leverage its diversified platform for long-term value creation. Management expressed confidence in the company’s undervalued stock price and its potential for future growth.

Looking ahead, Star Equity Holdings is well-positioned to drive shareholder value through a balanced approach of organic growth, disciplined capital allocation, and strategic acquisitions. The company remains committed to enhancing its market position and delivering sustainable, profitable expansion.

Sally Beauty Holdings, Inc. is a global leader in professional hair color, selling and distributing beauty supplies through its Sally Beauty Supply and Beauty Systems Group segments. The company offers a wide range of products for hair, nails, and skin care, catering to both professional and retail consumers.

In its latest earnings report, Sally Beauty Holdings announced a modest increase in fourth-quarter consolidated net sales by 1.3%, with comparable sales also rising by 1.3%. The company reported a significant increase in GAAP diluted EPS by 7% and adjusted diluted EPS by 10% for the quarter, reflecting strong financial performance.

Key highlights from the report include a full-year GAAP diluted EPS increase of 32% and adjusted diluted EPS growth of 12%. The company generated $275 million in cash flow from operations, which was used to invest in growth, strengthen the balance sheet, and return value to shareholders. Additionally, Sally Beauty provided fiscal 2026 guidance and long-term financial targets, indicating continued focus on growth and profitability.

The company’s strategic initiatives have resulted in robust performance across both its Sally and BSG segments, with a notable expansion in operating margins. Despite a slight decline in full-year net sales, the company maintained healthy gross margins and improved its operating earnings.

Looking ahead, Sally Beauty Holdings remains optimistic about its future prospects, supported by resilient customer demand and strategic initiatives aimed at driving growth and profitability. The company’s fiscal 2026 guidance and long-term targets reflect confidence in its ability to achieve sustained financial performance.

U.S. Global Investors, Inc. is a registered investment advisory firm based in San Antonio, Texas, specializing in niche markets and offering investment management services to U.S. Global Investors Funds and ETFs.

In the first quarter of fiscal 2026, U.S. Global Investors reported a significant rebound in profitability, with net income reaching $1.5 million, marking a 378% increase from the same period in the previous year. This turnaround was primarily driven by a substantial rise in investment income, which saw a nearly 300% increase from the previous quarter. Operating revenues also showed growth, rising by 15% from the June quarter.

Key financial metrics highlighted in the report include average assets under management of $1.4 billion and a shareholder yield of 8.32%, which is notably higher than the yield on five-year and 10-year Treasury bonds. The company benefited from the surge in gold prices, which led to positive fund flows into its gold mining and natural resource funds, reversing the negative trend from the previous year.

Looking ahead, U.S. Global Investors is optimistic about its positioning in the current economic and geopolitical climate. The company sees potential in gold and gold mining stocks as global debt levels rise, and it continues to offer diverse investment options, including exposure to high-tech sectors through its ETFs.

The company maintains a strong liquidity position with $24.6 million in cash and cash equivalents, ensuring it can meet its current obligations. The management’s strategic initiatives, including a share repurchase program and monthly dividends, aim to enhance shareholder value.

data.title: Cantaloupe, Inc. Reports Revenue Growth Amid Merger Plans
data.description:
Cantaloupe, Inc. ( (CTLP) ) has released its Q1 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Cantaloupe, Inc. presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Cantaloupe, Inc. is a digital payments and software services company that provides end-to-end technology solutions for self-service commerce, operating primarily in the financial technology sector. The company offers a single platform for integrated payments processing and software solutions, serving a diverse range of industries including vending, micro-markets, and smart retail.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, Cantaloupe, Inc. reported total revenues of $80.9 million, marking a significant increase from $70.8 million in the same quarter of the previous year. Despite this revenue growth, the company experienced a net loss of $919,000 compared to a net income of $3.6 million in the prior year, attributed to increased operating expenses and merger-related costs.

Key financial highlights include a 10% increase in subscription and transaction fees, reaching $70.3 million, and a notable rise in equipment sales to $10.5 million. However, the company’s operating expenses surged to $31.9 million, driven by merger, acquisition, and integration expenses, which significantly impacted profitability. Additionally, the company is navigating through a merger with 365 Retail Markets, LLC, which is expected to close in the first half of 2026.

Looking ahead, Cantaloupe, Inc. remains focused on expanding its market presence and enhancing its technology offerings. The management is optimistic about the merger’s potential to create synergies and drive future growth, although the completion of the merger is contingent upon regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions.

data.title: Immunic, Inc. Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Immunic, Inc. ( (IMUX) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Immunic, Inc. presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Immunic, Inc. is a late-stage biotechnology company focused on developing novel oral therapies for neurologic and gastrointestinal diseases. Its lead program, vidofludimus calcium, is in phase 3 trials for relapsing multiple sclerosis and has shown promise in earlier phase 2 trials.

In its third-quarter 2025 financial results, Immunic highlighted significant clinical data for vidofludimus calcium, particularly its potential to improve disability in progressive multiple sclerosis. The company also reported a new patent allowance, enhancing its intellectual property position.

Key financial metrics showed a decrease in research and development expenses for the quarter, while general and administrative expenses increased. The company reported a net loss of $25.6 million for the quarter, with cash and cash equivalents totaling $35.1 million, indicating a need for additional capital to sustain operations beyond 12 months.

Immunic’s management remains optimistic about vidofludimus calcium’s potential, anticipating top-line data from phase 3 trials by the end of 2026. The company is also preparing for further clinical testing of its other pipeline candidate, IMU-856, contingent on securing additional funding.

Looking ahead, Immunic aims to leverage its strong clinical data and intellectual property to advance its pipeline and address unmet needs in the multiple sclerosis market, while managing its financial resources to support ongoing and future development activities.

data.title: CytoSorbents Reports Strong Q3 2025 Growth and Strategic Advances
data.description:

CytoSorbents Corporation is a company specializing in blood purification technologies, primarily used in intensive care units and cardiac surgery to treat life-threatening conditions. In its third-quarter 2025 financial report, CytoSorbents announced a 10% year-over-year revenue increase, reaching $9.5 million, driven by strong performance in distributor territories and direct sales outside Germany. The company also reported an improved gross margin of 70% and a reduced operating loss of $2.9 million compared to the previous year.

Key highlights from the report include an amended credit agreement with Avenue Capital Group, providing an additional $2.5 million in funding and extending the interest-only period, as well as the implementation of a Workforce and Cost Reduction Program aimed at achieving cash-flow breakeven by Q1 2026. Additionally, CytoSorbents submitted a pre-submission package for DrugSorb-ATR to the FDA, with a regulatory decision anticipated by mid-2026.

The company’s strategic initiatives and financial performance reflect a positive trajectory, with expectations of improved performance in the German market and continued growth in other regions. The recent clinical data publications further support the effectiveness of CytoSorbents’ technologies in managing critical conditions.

Looking ahead, CytoSorbents remains focused on advancing its strategic initiatives, including the potential approval and launch of DrugSorb-ATR in the US, and achieving cash-flow breakeven in early 2026. The company is optimistic about its ability to sustain growth and innovation in the blood purification sector.

Vuzix Corporation, a company specializing in the development of wearable display technology, primarily operates in the smart glasses and augmented reality sector. In its latest earnings report, Vuzix Corporation highlighted a challenging financial period with a net loss of $23.7 million for the nine months ending September 30, 2025. The company’s total sales decreased to $4.04 million from $4.48 million in the same period last year, with both product sales and engineering services experiencing declines. data.title: Banco do Brasil S.A. Reports Decline in Net Income
data.description:
Banco do Brasil S.A. ( (BDORY) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Banco do Brasil S.A. presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Banco do Brasil S.A. is a leading financial institution in Brazil, primarily engaged in providing a wide range of banking services, including credit operations, asset management, and insurance, with a strong presence in the agribusiness sector.

In its latest earnings report, Banco do Brasil S.A. reported an adjusted net income of R$ 14.9 billion for the first nine months of 2025, marking a 47.2% decrease compared to the same period in 2024. The bank’s performance was influenced by various factors, including changes in financial revenues and expenses, as well as adjustments in credit costs and fee income.

Key financial metrics highlighted in the report include a net interest income of R$ 75.3 billion for the nine-month period, with a quarterly increase in financial revenues offsetting higher financial expenses. The cost of credit rose significantly by 66.4% year-over-year, reaching R$ 44.0 billion, driven by increased delinquency rates, particularly in the agricultural portfolio. Fee income totaled R$ 26.0 billion, supported by positive performance in asset management and insurance-related fees.

The bank’s administrative expenses increased by 5.4% year-over-year, reflecting salary adjustments and continued investments in technology and cybersecurity. The expanded credit portfolio showed a 7.5% growth over the past year, with notable increases in credit lines for individuals and companies. However, delinquency indicators rose, with the 90-day delinquency rate reaching 4.93% by the end of September 2025.

Looking ahead, Banco do Brasil S.A. remains focused on its strategic initiatives, aiming to balance growth with risk management. The management continues to monitor market conditions and adjust its strategies to maintain financial stability and support its diverse client base.

data.title: Edgewell Personal Care Reports Mixed Fiscal 2025 Results
data.description:
Edgewell Personal Care Co ( (EPC) ) has released its Q4 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Edgewell Personal Care Co presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Edgewell Personal Care Co, a leading consumer products company, operates in the personal care sector with a diverse portfolio of brands including Schick, Wilkinson Sword, and Banana Boat. The company recently released its fourth-quarter and fiscal year 2025 earnings report, highlighting a mixed financial performance. While fourth-quarter net sales increased by 3.8%, the fiscal year saw a 1.3% decrease in net sales. The company returned $120 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

Key financial metrics from the report include a GAAP EPS of $0.53 and an adjusted EPS of $2.52 for fiscal 2025, despite a challenging year marked by internal and external pressures. The company ended the year with $225.7 million in cash and a net debt leverage ratio of 3.9x. Additionally, Edgewell announced the sale of its Feminine Care business to Essity for $340 million, a strategic move to focus on its core personal care operations.

Segment-wise, the Wet Shave segment saw a slight decline in organic net sales, while Sun and Skin Care experienced significant growth. However, the Feminine Care segment faced challenges, with a notable decrease in segment profit. The company also recorded a non-cash goodwill impairment charge of $51.1 million related to the Feminine Care business divestiture.

Looking ahead, Edgewell’s management is optimistic about returning to organic sales growth in fiscal 2026, with expectations of accelerated growth in the latter half of the year. The company plans to focus on execution, margin recovery, and sustainable shareholder value, leveraging its streamlined operations and revitalized brand portfolio.

data.title: Relmada Therapeutics Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results
data.description:

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions

Relmada Therapeutics Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing innovative treatments for neurological and psychological disorders, with its primary operations in the pharmaceutical industry. The company is known for its work on novel therapies such as NDV-01 and Sepranolone, targeting conditions like non-muscle invasive bladder cancer and Prader-Willi Syndrome, respectively.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, Relmada Therapeutics Inc. highlighted significant financial challenges, reporting a net loss of $10.1 million for the quarter and $37.5 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. The company’s cash and cash equivalents decreased significantly, reflecting ongoing operational expenses and strategic investments in its development pipeline.

Key financial metrics indicate a reduction in operating expenses compared to the previous year, with research and development expenses dropping from $11.1 million to $4 million for the quarter. Despite these reductions, the company continues to face substantial losses, primarily driven by its commitment to advancing clinical trials and strategic acquisitions, such as the purchase of Sepranolone from Asarina Pharma AB.

Looking forward, Relmada Therapeutics Inc. has taken steps to strengthen its financial position, including a recent underwritten offering that raised approximately $94 million. This capital infusion is expected to support the company’s operations and development activities for at least the next 12 months. Management remains focused on advancing its clinical programs and exploring strategic partnerships to enhance its pipeline and drive future growth.

data.title: OTE Group Reports Strong Q3 2025 Results
data.description:
Hellenic Telecom Organization S.A. (ADR) ( (HLTOY) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Hellenic Telecom Organization S.A. (ADR) presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Hellenic Telecom Organization S.A. (ADR), known as OTE Group, is Greece’s largest telecommunications provider, offering a comprehensive range of services including fixed-line and mobile telephony, broadband, pay-TV, and ICT solutions. In its latest earnings report for the third quarter of 2025, OTE Group highlighted a successful strategic shift with the disposal of Telekom Romania Mobile, resulting in significant free cash flow savings and an extraordinary dividend for shareholders.

The company’s financial performance in Greece showed positive momentum, with a 5.0% increase in revenues and a 2.0% rise in Adjusted EBITDA (AL). Mobile service revenues grew by 2.7%, supported by a 6.4% increase in post-paid subscribers. Fixed retail service revenues also saw growth, driven by double-digit increases in TV services and a strong expansion in Fiber-To-The-Home (FTTH) customer base, which reached 509,000.

Key financial metrics revealed a 36.0% increase in profit to owners of the parent, with earnings per share rising by 39.5%. Despite a slight decline in EBIT due to higher amortization costs, the company maintained a robust free cash flow of €107.6 million for the quarter. OTE’s strategic focus on expanding its FTTH and 5G networks is evident, with plans to reach 3.5 million homes by 2030 and a continued emphasis on enhancing its mobile network capabilities.

Looking ahead, OTE Group is poised to maintain its market leadership in Greece through ongoing investments in advanced networks and a strong service portfolio. The company expects sustained growth, driven by the uptake of FTTH, the introduction of Fixed Wireless Access (FWA), and the expansion of TV services. OTE remains committed to delivering value to shareholders and customers, with a focus on service excellence and digital transformation.

data.title: ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE Reports Q3 2025 Earnings
data.description:
ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE ( (PBSFY) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE is a prominent media company based in Germany, primarily operating in the entertainment sector with a focus on television broadcasting and digital platforms, including its streaming service Joyn. The company is known for its diverse portfolio of TV channels and digital content offerings, catering to a wide audience in the German-speaking region.

In the third quarter of 2025, ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE reported a decline in revenues, reflecting the challenging economic environment and strategic portfolio adjustments. Despite a 7% drop in group revenues to EUR 820 million, the company saw significant growth in its streaming platform Joyn, with AVoD advertising revenues increasing by 42%. However, the decline in TV advertising revenues and the deconsolidation of Verivox impacted overall financial performance.

Key financial metrics showed a decrease in adjusted EBITDA by 27% to EUR 76 million for the third quarter, primarily due to the weaker TV advertising market. The company’s strategic focus on digital transformation and portfolio management was evident, with the sale of non-core assets like Verivox and investments in digital growth areas such as podcasts and streaming. MFE-MEDIAFOREUROPE N.V. became the majority shareholder, enhancing ProSiebenSat.1’s position in the European entertainment market.

Looking ahead, ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE remains committed to its revenue forecast for 2025, with expectations of group revenues between EUR 3.65 billion and EUR 3.80 billion. The company is focusing on digital growth and cost efficiency to navigate the challenging market conditions, with a specified outlook for adjusted EBITDA between EUR 420 million and EUR 450 million. The strategic changes and new leadership appointments are aimed at strengthening the company’s market position and driving long-term growth.

data.description:
Global Water Resources ( (GWRS) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Global Water Resources presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Global Water Resources, Inc. is a water resource management company based in Phoenix, Arizona, primarily engaged in providing water, wastewater, and recycled water services. The company operates within the regulated utility sector, focusing on sustainable water management practices.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, Global Water Resources reported a slight increase in total revenue compared to the previous year, driven by growth in both water and wastewater services. However, the company experienced a decline in net income due to increased operating expenses.

Key financial highlights include a 8.4% rise in total revenue to $15.5 million, with water service revenue increasing by 13.2% and wastewater service revenue growing by 3.1%. Despite the revenue growth, operating income decreased by 26.7% to $2.9 million, primarily due to higher operations and maintenance costs, as well as increased general and administrative expenses.

The company continues to expand its footprint, as evidenced by the acquisition of seven water systems from Tucson Water, which added approximately 2,200 service connections. This strategic acquisition is expected to enhance the company’s long-term growth prospects and operational efficiency.

Looking ahead, Global Water Resources remains focused on expanding its service areas and improving operational efficiencies. The management is optimistic about future growth opportunities, supported by ongoing regulatory developments and strategic acquisitions.

data.title: Grieg Seafood ASA Navigates Strategic Transformation
data.description:
Grieg Seafood ASA ( (GRGSF) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Grieg Seafood ASA presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Grieg Seafood ASA is a prominent player in the aquaculture industry, focusing on salmon farming primarily in Norway. The company is characterized by its strategic emphasis on post-smolt production and sustainable aquaculture practices.

In its latest earnings report for the third quarter of 2025, Grieg Seafood ASA highlighted several significant developments, including the approval of the divestment of its operations in Finnmark and Canada by Norwegian and US authorities. The company reported a harvest volume of 6,820 tonnes, a decrease from the previous year, and faced challenges due to high seawater temperatures affecting growth rates.

Key financial metrics revealed a decrease in sales revenues to NOK 754 million, down from NOK 856 million in the same quarter last year. The operational EBIT per kilogram dropped to NOK 3.2 from NOK 8.8, reflecting increased production costs and lower harvest weights. The company also implemented a significant reduction in headquarters staff to improve cost efficiency.

Looking ahead, Grieg Seafood is focused on completing its divestment transaction, reducing costs, and optimizing its capital structure. The company plans to strengthen its financial platform with a new bank syndicate and aims to maintain a disciplined approach to capital allocation, prioritizing shareholder returns and sustainable growth.

Overall, Grieg Seafood ASA is navigating a transformative period with strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing operational efficiency and positioning itself as a resilient player in the aquaculture sector, with a focus on long-term value creation for shareholders.

KBC Group is a prominent financial institution operating in the banking and insurance sectors, known for its innovative digital solutions and strong presence in European markets.

In the third quarter of 2025, KBC Group reported a net profit of 1,002 million euros, showcasing a robust financial performance. The company experienced growth in key income streams such as net interest income and insurance revenues, while maintaining stable customer deposits and expanding its loan portfolio.

Key highlights from the earnings report include a 10% year-on-year increase in net interest income, a significant rise in insurance service results, and a 6% growth in net fee and commission income. The company also maintained a strong solvency position with a common equity ratio of 14.9% and a liquidity coverage ratio of 158%. KBC’s digital innovation continues to be a focal point, with advancements in its AI-powered digital assistant, Kate, and the development of a euro stablecoin in collaboration with other European banks.

Looking ahead, KBC Group remains optimistic about its financial outlook, with an updated guidance for 2025 projecting at least a 7.5% increase in total income and a net interest income of at least 5.95 billion euros. The company is committed to further enhancing its digital offerings and expanding its market presence through strategic acquisitions, while maintaining a strong capital position.

MarineMax, Inc. is the world’s largest recreational boat and yacht retailer, marina operator, and superyacht services company, offering a wide range of marine products and services globally.

In its fiscal 2025 fourth quarter and full year results, MarineMax reported a fourth-quarter revenue of $552.2 million, with a gross margin expansion to 34.7% due to its diversified portfolio. Despite a challenging retail environment, the company saw a 2.3% increase in same-store sales, and the Fort Lauderdale Boat Show set a post-COVID record for unit sales and revenue gains.

Key financial highlights for the fourth quarter include a net loss of $0.9 million and an adjusted EBITDA of $17.3 million. For the full fiscal year 2025, MarineMax reported a revenue of $2.3 billion, a net loss of $31.6 million, and an adjusted EBITDA of $109.8 million. The company has been focusing on strategic expansion into higher-margin businesses, which has contributed to its improved gross margins.

Looking ahead, MarineMax has provided guidance for fiscal 2026, expecting adjusted EBITDA to be between $110 million and $125 million, with adjusted net income ranging from $0.40 to $0.95 per diluted share. The company remains confident in its long-term strategy, emphasizing diversification, product innovation, and digital engagement to navigate industry cycles and enhance shareholder value.

AtkinsRéalis Group Inc. is a global engineering services and nuclear company, known for its comprehensive solutions in engineering, project management, and sustainable infrastructure development. Headquartered in Montreal, the company operates across various strategic sectors including engineering services, nuclear, and capital projects.

In its third quarter of 2025, AtkinsRéalis reported a robust financial performance with a 15% increase in total revenue, reaching $2.8 billion. The company also achieved a record high backlog of $21 billion, reflecting a 20% growth from the previous year. Additionally, the company announced the acquisition of C2AE, furthering its expansion strategy in the United States.

Key financial highlights for the quarter include a 17% increase in AtkinsRéalis Services revenue and a 68% rise in diluted earnings per share from professional services and project management. The company’s net income attributable to shareholders rose by 41.5% to $146.7 million. The nuclear segment showed remarkable growth with a 61.7% increase in revenue, while the Linxon segment saw an 18.8% rise.

Despite the positive results, the company has revised its 2025 outlook for organic revenue growth in its Engineering Services Regions to a low-single digit percentage, citing lower than expected growth in certain segments. However, it has raised its nuclear revenue outlook for the year, indicating strong performance in that sector.

Looking ahead, AtkinsRéalis remains optimistic about its growth prospects, supported by its strategic acquisitions and strong backlog. The company aims to continue leveraging its unique mix of engineering and nuclear capabilities to meet the increasing demand for sustainable infrastructure and energy solutions globally.

data.title: Taseko Mines Reports Strong Q3 with Positive Outlook
data.description:
Taseko Mines ( (TGB) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Taseko Mines presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Taseko Mines Limited is a mining company primarily engaged in the extraction and production of copper and molybdenum, with operations centered in North America. The company is noted for its Gibraltar and Florence Copper projects, which are key contributors to its copper production capabilities.

In its latest earnings report, Taseko Mines announced a third-quarter adjusted EBITDA of $62 million, despite a net loss of $28 million. The company reported revenues of $174 million, generated from the sale of 26 million pounds of copper and 421 thousand pounds of molybdenum. Notably, copper production at the Gibraltar mine improved significantly, with expectations for further increases in the coming quarter.

Key highlights from the report include the completion of significant milestones at the Florence Copper project, where the SX/EW plant is nearing completion and is expected to produce its first copper cathode early next year. The company also strengthened its financial position through a $173 million equity financing, which was used to repay debt and support ongoing projects. Additionally, Taseko’s Gibraltar mine saw improved copper grades and recoveries, contributing to a reduction in operating costs.

Looking ahead, Taseko Mines remains optimistic about its growth prospects, with plans to increase copper production and further develop its projects, including the Yellowhead copper project. The company anticipates strong copper prices in 2026, driven by increasing demand and limited supply, which could positively impact its financial performance.

data.title: Trevi Therapeutics Reports Q3 2025 Financials and Updates
data.description:
Trevi Therapeutics ( (TRVI) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Trevi Therapeutics presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Trevi Therapeutics, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing Haduvio™, an investigational therapy for chronic cough in patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF), non-IPF interstitial lung disease, and refractory chronic cough.

In its third-quarter 2025 financial report, Trevi Therapeutics announced its plans to initiate a comprehensive Phase 3 program for chronic cough in IPF patients by the first half of 2026. The company ended the quarter with $194.9 million in cash and marketable securities, projecting a cash runway extending into 2028.

Key highlights from the report include the successful completion of a Phase 1 drug-drug interaction study and positive safety reviews from the TIDAL study. The company is preparing for an End-of-Phase 2 meeting with the FDA, reflecting its strategic focus on advancing Haduvio for chronic cough conditions that currently lack approved therapies.

Financially, Trevi reported a net loss of $11.8 million for the third quarter, a slight improvement from the $13.2 million loss in the same period last year. R&D expenses decreased due to reduced clinical development costs, while G&A expenses rose due to increased professional fees and personnel costs.

Looking ahead, Trevi Therapeutics remains committed to advancing its clinical programs and engaging with regulatory authorities to address the unmet needs in chronic cough treatment, with a clear path for growth outlined by its management.

Terna S.p.A. Unsponsored ADR is a leading Italian electricity transmission company, focusing on the development and maintenance of the national grid, with a strong emphasis on supporting the energy transition and enhancing energy security.

In the first nine months of 2025, Terna reported significant financial growth, with a notable increase in capital expenditure exceeding €2 billion, reflecting its commitment to infrastructure development. The company’s revenue rose by 8.9% to €2,882.3 million, driven by both regulated and non-regulated activities, while EBITDA surpassed €2 billion, marking a 7.1% increase.

Key financial highlights include a 4.9% rise in group net profit to €852.7 million and a 22.9% increase in capital expenditure compared to the previous year. Terna’s strategic projects, such as the Tyrrhenian Link and the Adriatic Link, are progressing well, contributing to the company’s robust performance. Additionally, the successful MACSE auction and the acquisition of Rete 2 S.r.l. highlight Terna’s strategic initiatives to enhance grid security and efficiency.

Looking ahead, Terna remains focused on its 2024-2028 Industrial Plan, aiming to integrate renewable sources and improve grid resilience. The company plans to continue its investment in key projects, such as the Tyrrhenian and Adriatic Links, while maintaining a sustainable approach to business operations.

Overall, Terna’s strategic investments and operational efficiency improvements position the company well for continued growth and contribution to Italy’s energy transition goals.

Sera Prognostics, Inc., a health diagnostics company headquartered in Salt Lake City, Utah, specializes in precision pregnancy care by offering innovative biomarker information to improve maternal and neonatal health. The company has recently reported its financial results for the third quarter of 2025, highlighting significant progress in payer initiatives and strategic leadership appointments.

In the third quarter of 2025, Sera Prognostics made notable advancements, including launching a Medicaid pilot in Nevada and engaging with payers across thirteen states. The company is poised to publish full study results of its PRIME study, which is expected to demonstrate the health economic benefits of its PreTRM Test. Additionally, Sera has strengthened its leadership team with the appointments of Dr. Tiffany Inglis as Chief Medical Officer and Marisol Urbano as Head of Commercial Operations.

Financially, Sera Prognostics reported a revenue of $16,000 for the third quarter of 2025, a decrease from $29,000 in the same period of 2024. Operating expenses slightly increased to $9.0 million, while the net loss for the quarter was $7.8 million, a slight improvement from the previous year’s $7.9 million loss. The company holds cash and equivalents of approximately $102.4 million, expected to support its operations through significant milestones until 2028.

Looking ahead, Sera Prognostics is focused on driving adoption of its PreTRM Test and expanding its market engagement. The company is actively pursuing broader coverage at the employer, plan, and state levels to transform prenatal care and deliver long-term value for shareholders. With ongoing discussions and strategic initiatives, Sera is well-positioned to make a meaningful impact in the maternal and neonatal health sector.

data.title: Enel SpA Reports Mixed Q3 2025 Performance
data.description:
Enel SpA Unsponsored ADR ( (ENLAY) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Enel SpA Unsponsored ADR presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Enel SpA, a leading multinational energy company based in Italy, operates primarily in the electricity and gas sectors, with a significant focus on renewable energy sources. The company has a diverse portfolio that includes renewables, nuclear, and thermal energy, and it is known for its commitment to sustainability and innovation in the energy industry.

In its latest earnings report for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2025, Enel SpA highlighted its continued efforts in expanding its renewable energy capacity. The company reported a 3.9% increase in total installed capacity, reaching 92.9 GW, with renewables accounting for a significant portion of this growth. Despite a slight decline in total production, Enel remains focused on enhancing its emission-free energy production.

Key financial metrics from the report indicate a mixed performance. While the total installed capacity saw a positive trend, total production decreased by 2.9% year-over-year. Renewables production also faced a slight decline, although the company made notable advancements in battery energy storage systems (BESS), which grew by 41.7%. The electricity distribution in Spain showed a positive growth of 3.1%, contrasting with a slight decline in Italy.

Enel’s strategic moves include the acquisition of a majority stake in Cetasa, which increased its wind capacity in Spain by 99 MW. This acquisition aligns with Enel’s strategy to bolster its renewable energy portfolio and expand its market presence in Europe.

Looking ahead, Enel’s management remains optimistic about the company’s growth prospects, particularly in the renewable energy sector. The company continues to invest in sustainable energy solutions and infrastructure, aiming to enhance its operational efficiency and expand its global footprint.

data.description:
Nortech ( (NSYS) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Nortech presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Nortech Systems Incorporated is a prominent provider of engineering and manufacturing solutions for complex electromedical and electromechanical products, primarily serving the medical imaging, medical device, industrial, and aerospace & defense markets. Headquartered in Maple Grove, Minnesota, the company operates across the U.S., Latin America, and Asia, and is listed on the NASDAQ Stock Market under the symbol NSYS.

In the third quarter of 2025, Nortech Systems reported net sales of $30.5 million, a slight decrease from the previous year’s $31.4 million. Despite this, the company significantly reduced its net loss to $146 thousand compared to $739 thousand in the same quarter of 2024. The adjusted EBITDA saw a substantial increase to $1.3 million from $0.1 million in the prior year, indicating improved operational efficiency.

Key highlights from the quarter include a 31% increase in gross profit and a 90-day backlog of $31.3 million, up from $29.6 million the previous year. The company also achieved a significant regulatory milestone with its Monterrey, Mexico facility obtaining AS9100:D certification, enhancing its service offerings in the aerospace and industrial markets.

Looking ahead, Nortech Systems’ management remains optimistic about leveraging its operational performance and strategic positioning in the nearshoring landscape. The company’s focus on manufacturing efficiencies and regulatory achievements positions it well for future growth in its key markets.

data.title: Sapiens International Reports Strong Q3 Revenue Growth
data.description:
Sapiens International Corporation ( (SPNS) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Sapiens International Corporation presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Sapiens International Corporation is a global leader in providing intelligent software solutions for the insurance industry, empowering insurers with AI-driven innovation and digital transformation capabilities across various insurance sectors.

In its third quarter of 2025, Sapiens International Corporation reported a notable 11.2% increase in revenue, reaching $152.3 million, compared to the same period last year. This growth was driven by strong performance across all geographic markets, particularly in North America and other regions. The company also highlighted a significant increase in its annualized recurring revenue, which rose by 26.7% year-over-year.

Key financial metrics for the quarter included a gross profit of $67.3 million, reflecting an 11.6% increase from the previous year. However, the company’s operating income and net income saw declines of 18.3% and 22.0% respectively, attributed to increased operating expenses. Despite these challenges, Sapiens maintained a non-GAAP operating margin of 16.7% and continued to secure new deals and strengthen customer relationships globally.

Looking ahead, Sapiens remains focused on advancing its platform, accelerating cloud adoption, and expanding its global footprint. The company’s strategic initiatives are aimed at sustaining growth and operational efficiency, leveraging AI-driven innovation to support insurers in their digital transformation journey.

data.title: Ultrapar Reports Strong Q3 2025 Financial Performance
data.description:
Ultrapar Participacoes ( (UGP) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Ultrapar Participacoes presented to its investors.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Ultrapar Participações S.A. is a Brazilian company engaged in the energy, mobility, and logistics infrastructure sectors through its subsidiaries Ultragaz, Ipiranga, Ultracargo, and Hidrovias do Brasil. The company operates in the energy sector and has a strong presence in logistics and fuel distribution.

In the third quarter of 2025, Ultrapar reported a net revenue of R$ 37.1 billion, with an adjusted EBITDA of R$ 1.9 billion and a net income of R$ 0.8 billion. The company showed strong cash generation from operations totaling R$ 2.1 billion, and investments amounted to R$ 756 million.

Key highlights from the quarter include record results from Hidrovias, a reduction in leverage from 1.9x to 1.7x, and the completion of strategic projects such as the expansion of the Santos terminal and the sale of the coastal navigation operation. Ultrapar also signed an agreement to acquire a 37.5% stake in Virtu Participações, reinforcing its investment strategy in high-growth sectors.

Ultrapar’s management remains focused on enhancing operational efficiency and strategic positioning. The company continues to combat irregularities in the fuel sector and has made significant progress in its growth agenda, including the approval of the LPG port terminal in Pecém.

Looking ahead, Ultrapar aims to maintain its financial strength and strategic growth initiatives, leveraging its diversified portfolio to create value and capitalize on opportunities in the energy and logistics sectors.

data.title: Director’s Bold Move: Major Stock Purchase Signals Confidence in Cooper-Standard Holdings
data.description:
New insider activity at Cooper-Standard Holdings ( (CPS) ) has taken place on November 13, 2025.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Director David John Mastrocola has made a significant investment in Cooper-Standard Holdings by purchasing 2,000 shares of the company’s stock. This transaction is valued at $58,100, indicating a strong vote of confidence in the company’s future prospects.

Recent Updates on CPS stock

In the last 24 hours, Cooper-Standard Holdings has seen a mixed bag of developments following its Q3 earnings call. The company reported strong operational performance and strategic advancements, particularly in the electric vehicle sector, but also faced challenges such as a net loss and anticipated disruptions in the fourth quarter due to aluminum supply chain issues. Despite these hurdles, Cooper-Standard demonstrated impressive cost optimization, securing $18 million in savings and a 140 basis point improvement in gross margin. The company’s strategic alignment with electric vehicles, with 83% of new business awards related to battery electric or hybrid platforms, positions it favorably in the market. Additionally, Stifel raised Cooper-Standard’s price target, citing the successful pass-through of increased pricing to customers and the accretive impact of tariffs on EPS. Overall, while Cooper-Standard faces rising costs and market headwinds, its strategic initiatives and operational excellence reflect resilience and adaptability.

Spark’s Take on CPS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CPS is a Neutral.

Cooper-Standard Holdings’ overall stock score reflects a mixed financial performance with improved revenue but low profitability and a weak balance sheet. Technical indicators suggest bearish momentum, while the valuation is fair. The earnings call provided a balanced view with operational improvements but highlighted significant challenges ahead.
